name: sr_optout_hinglish_keyword_honored
notes: config/opt_out_keywords.yaml's literal Hinglish member 'band karo' hits pre_filter_gate Rule a (matches_opt_out_or_dsr, boundary-safe containment) -> opt_out_handler, which freezes autonomy + cancels every open batch atomically (autonomy.kill_autonomy_by_keyword, VT-384 gate-bounce F1) THEN sends the team_opt_out_confirmation TEMPLATE with tenant_id (so it IS conversation_log-logged, unlike the freeform ENABLE/KILL handlers). Distinct from canaries/scenarios/stop_intent_natural.json (ambiguous English phrasing, marked expected_fail) and stop_resume.json (a Hinglish phrase deliberately NOT in the literal keyword list) -- 'band karo' is a real, WORKING keyword match, not a known gap. Setup: `setup --onboarded` then `script <tenant> sr_optout_hinglish_keyword_honored.json`.

steps.message: bas ab customers ko koi bhi message mat bhejna, sab kuch band karo
steps.note: The literal Hinglish opt-out keyword 'band karo' hits the deterministic gate. VT-683 P1 (freeform-first, Fazal-ruled): the in-window confirmation is now the REAL stop-confirm text ('Got it — I've stopped all messaging…'), not the '[template: team_opt_out_confirmation]' marker (the template stays the out-of-window belt) -- 'stopped' asserts the confirmation class on the live copy.
